rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type annexes in the component type scope, and implementation annexes in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope when writing AGREE contracts
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope to maintain architectural integrity
Ensure all identifiers used in AGREE contracts are visible in the current scope
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
Place implementation-level bindings in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
For input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees, use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Do not use range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
